title: pastinaca humilis
intro: Pastinaca humilis, also known as wild parsley or wild parsnip, is a perennial plant species in the carrot family that is native to Europe and Asia. The plant contains furanocoumarins, which have been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. Consuming Pastinaca humilis has been associated with various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. The plant's roots and leaves can be used to make herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, which are often used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
